children.heading: Definition
children.label: 1 Definition
children.text: The term “group health plan” means an employee welfare benefit plan (as defined in section 3(1) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 []) to the extent that the plan provides medical care (as defined in paragraph (2)) and including items and services paid for as medical care) to employees or their dependents (as defined under the terms of the plan) directly or through insurance, reimbursement, or otherwise. Except for purposes of part C of title XI of the Social Security Act ( et seq.), such term shall not include any qualified small employer health reimbursement arrangement (as defined in ).29 U.S.C. 1002(1)42 U.S.C. 1320dsection 9831(d)(2) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: the diagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease, or amounts paid for the purpose of affecting any structure or function of the body,

children.label: B
children.text: amounts paid for transportation primarily for and essential to medical care referred to in subparagraph (A), and

children.label: C
children.text: amounts paid for insurance covering medical care referred to in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Treatment of certain plans as group health plan for notice provision
children.label: 3 Treatment of certain plans as group health plan for notice provision
children.text: A program under which creditable coverage described in subparagraph (C), (D), (E), or (F) of section 2701(c)(1) is provided shall be treated as a group health plan for purposes of applying section 2701(e).11See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Definitions relating to health insurance
children.label: b Definitions relating to health insurance

children.heading: Health insurance coverage
children.label: 1 Health insurance coverage
children.text: The term “health insurance coverage” means benefits consisting of medical care (provided directly, through insurance or reimbursement, or otherwise and including items and services paid for as medical care) under any hospital or medical service policy or certificate, hospital or medical service plan contract, or health maintenance organization contract offered by a health insurance issuer.

children.heading: Health insurance issuer
children.label: 2 Health insurance issuer
children.text: The term “health insurance issuer” means an insurance company, insurance service, or insurance organization (including a health maintenance organization, as defined in paragraph (3)) which is licensed to engage in the business of insurance in a State and which is subject to State law which regulates insurance (within the meaning of section 514(b)(2) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 []). Such term does not include a group health plan.29 U.S.C. 1144(b)(2)

children.label: A
children.text: a Federally qualified health maintenance organization (as defined in ),section 300e(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: an organization recognized under State law as a health maintenance organization, or

children.label: C
children.text: a similar organization regulated under State law for solvency in the same manner and to the same extent as such a health maintenance organization.

children.heading: Group health insurance coverage
children.label: 4 Group health insurance coverage
children.text: The term “group health insurance coverage” means, in connection with a group health plan, health insurance coverage offered in connection with such plan.

children.heading: Individual health insurance coverage
children.label: 5 Individual health insurance coverage
children.text: The term “individual health insurance coverage” means health insurance coverage offered to individuals in the individual market, but does not include short-term limited duration insurance.

children.label: A
children.text: Coverage only for accident, or disability income insurance, or any combination thereof.

children.label: B
children.text: Coverage issued as a supplement to liability insurance.

children.label: C
children.text: Liability insurance, including general liability insurance and automobile liability insurance.

children.label: D
children.text: Workers’ compensation or similar insurance.

children.label: E
children.text: Automobile medical payment insurance.

children.label: F
children.text: Credit-only insurance.

children.label: G
children.text: Coverage for on-site medical clinics.

children.label: H
children.text: Other similar insurance coverage, specified in regulations, under which benefits for medical care are secondary or incidental to other insurance benefits.

children.heading: Benefits not subject to requirements if offered separately
children.label: 2 Benefits not subject to requirements if offered separately
A
children.text: Limited scope dental or vision benefits.

children.label: B
children.text: Benefits for long-term care, nursing home care, home health care, community-based care, or any combination thereof.

children.label: C
children.text: Such other similar, limited benefits as are specified in regulations.

children.heading: Benefits not subject to requirements if offered as independent, noncoordinated benefits
children.label: 3 Benefits not subject to requirements if offered as independent, noncoordinated benefits
A
children.text: Coverage only for a specified disease or illness.

children.label: B
children.text: Hospital indemnity or other fixed indemnity insurance.

children.heading: Benefits not subject to requirements if offered as separate insurance policy
children.label: 4 Benefits not subject to requirements if offered as separate insurance policy
children.text: Medicare supplemental health insurance (as defined under ), coverage supplemental to the coverage provided under chapter 55 of title 10, and similar supplemental coverage provided to coverage under a group health plan.section 1395ss(g)(1) of this title

children.heading: Applicable State authority
children.label: 1 Applicable State authority
children.text: The term “applicable State authority” means, with respect to a health insurance issuer in a State, the State insurance commissioner or official or officials designated by the State to enforce the requirements of this subchapter for the State involved with respect to such issuer.

children.heading: Beneficiary
children.label: 2 Beneficiary
children.text: The term “beneficiary” has the meaning given such term under section 3(8) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [].29 U.S.C. 1002(8)

children.label: A
children.text: has been actively in existence for at least 5 years;

children.label: B
children.text: has been formed and maintained in good faith for purposes other than obtaining insurance;

children.label: C
children.text: does not condition membership in the association on any health status-related factor relating to an individual (including an employee of an employer or a dependent of an employee);

children.label: D
children.text: makes health insurance coverage offered through the association available to all members regardless of any health status-related factor relating to such members (or individuals eligible for coverage through a member);

children.label: E
children.text: does not make health insurance coverage offered through the association available other than in connection with a member of the association; and

children.label: F
children.text: meets such additional requirements as may be imposed under State law.

children.label: A
children.text: , other than subsection (f)(1) of such section insofar as it relates to pediatric vaccines.Section 4980B of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: Part 6 of subtitle B of title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [ et seq.], other than section 609 of such Act [].29 U.S.C. 116129 U.S.C. 1169

children.label: C
children.text: Subchapter XX of this chapter.

children.heading: Employee
children.label: 5 Employee
children.text: The term “employee” has the meaning given such term under section 3(6) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [].29 U.S.C. 1002(6)

children.heading: Employer
children.label: 6 Employer
children.text: The term “employer” has the meaning given such term under section 3(5) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [], except that such term shall include only employers of two or more employees.29 U.S.C. 1002(5)

children.heading: Church plan
children.label: 7 Church plan
children.text: The term “church plan” has the meaning given such term under section 3(33) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [].29 U.S.C. 1002(33)

children.label: A
children.text: The term “governmental plan” has the meaning given such term under section 3(32) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [] and any Federal governmental plan.29 U.S.C. 1002(32)

children.heading: .—Federal governmental plan
children.label: B .—Federal governmental plan
children.text: The term “Federal governmental plan” means a governmental plan established or maintained for its employees by the Government of the United States or by any agency or instrumentality of such Government.

children.heading: .—Non-Federal governmental plan
children.label: C .—Non-Federal governmental plan
children.text: The term “non-Federal governmental plan” means a governmental plan that is not a Federal governmental plan.

children.heading: Health status-related factor
children.label: 9 Health status-related factor
children.text: The term “health status-related factor” means any of the factors described in section 2702(a)(1).1

children.heading: Network plan
children.label: 10 Network plan
children.text: The term “network plan” means health insurance coverage of a health insurance issuer under which the financing and delivery of medical care (including items and services paid for as medical care) are provided, in whole or in part, through a defined set of providers under contract with the issuer.

children.heading: Participant
children.label: 11 Participant
children.text: The term “participant” has the meaning given such term under section 3(7) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [].29 U.S.C. 1002(7)

children.heading: Placed for adoption defined
children.label: 12 Placed for adoption defined
children.text: The term “placement”, or being “placed”, for adoption, in connection with any placement for adoption of a child with any person, means the assumption and retention by such person of a legal obligation for total or partial support of such child in anticipation of adoption of such child. The child’s placement with such person terminates upon the termination of such legal obligation.

children.heading: Plan sponsor
children.label: 13 Plan sponsor
children.text: The term “plan sponsor” has the meaning given such term under section 3(16)(B) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [].29 U.S.C. 1002(16)(B)

children.heading: State
children.label: 14 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.label: A
children.text: a dependent (as such term is used for purposes of section 2701(f)(2)) of such individual; and1

children.label: B
children.text: any other individual who is a first-degree, second-degree, third-degree, or fourth-degree relative of such individual or of an individual described in subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: such individual’s genetic tests,

children.label: ii
children.text: the genetic tests of family members of such individual, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the manifestation of a disease or disorder in family members of such individual.

children.heading: Inclusion of genetic services and participation in genetic research
children.label: B Inclusion of genetic services and participation in genetic research
children.text: Such term includes, with respect to any individual, any request for, or receipt of, genetic services, or participation in clinical research which includes genetic services, by such individual or any family member of such individual.

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: C Exclusions
children.text: The term “genetic information” shall not include information about the sex or age of any individual.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “genetic test” means an analysis of human DNA, RNA, chromosomes, proteins, or metabolites, that detects genotypes, mutations, or chromosomal changes.

children.label: i
children.text: an analysis of proteins or metabolites that does not detect genotypes, mutations, or chromosomal changes; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an analysis of proteins or metabolites that is directly related to a manifested disease, disorder, or pathological condition that could reasonably be detected by a health care professional with appropriate training and expertise in the field of medicine involved.

children.label: A
children.text: a genetic test;

children.label: B
children.text: genetic counseling (including obtaining, interpreting, or assessing genetic information); or

children.label: C
children.text: genetic education.

children.label: A
children.text: rules for, or determination of, eligibility (including enrollment and continued eligibility) for benefits under the plan or coverage;

children.label: B
children.text: the computation of premium or contribution amounts under the plan or coverage;

children.label: C
children.text: the application of any pre-existing condition exclusion under the plan or coverage; and

children.label: D
children.text: other activities related to the creation, renewal, or replacement of a contract of health insurance or health benefits.

children.heading: Qualified health plan
children.label: 20 Qualified health plan
children.text: The term “qualified health plan” has the meaning given such term in .section 18021(a) of this title

children.heading: Exchange
children.label: 21 Exchange
children.text: The term “Exchange” means an American Health Benefit Exchange established under .section 18031 of this title

children.heading: Definitions relating to markets and small employers
children.label: e Definitions relating to markets and small employers

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “individual market” means the market for health insurance coverage offered to individuals other than in connection with a group health plan.

children.heading: Treatment of very small groups
children.label: B Treatment of very small groups

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), such terms includes coverage offered in connection with a group health plan that has fewer than two participants as current employees on the first day of the plan year.2So in original. Probably should be “term”.2

children.heading: State exception
children.label: ii State exception
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply in the case of a State that elects to regulate the coverage described in such clause as coverage in the small group market.

children.heading: Large employer
children.label: 2 Large employer
children.text: The term “large employer” means, in connection with a group health plan with respect to a calendar year and a plan year, an employer who employed an average of at least 51 employees on business days during the preceding calendar year and who employs at least 2 employees on the first day of the plan year.

children.heading: Large group market
children.label: 3 Large group market
children.text: The term “large group market” means the health insurance market under which individuals obtain health insurance coverage (directly or through any arrangement) on behalf of themselves (and their dependents) through a group health plan maintained by a large employer.

children.heading: Small employer
children.label: 4 Small employer
children.text: The term “small employer” means, in connection with a group health plan with respect to a calendar year and a plan year, an employer who employed an average of at least 1 but not more than 50 employees on business days during the preceding calendar year and who employs at least 1 employees on the first day of the plan year.3So in original.3

children.heading: Small group market
children.label: 5 Small group market
children.text: The term “small group market” means the health insurance market under which individuals obtain health insurance coverage (directly or through any arrangement) on behalf of themselves (and their dependents) through a group health plan maintained by a small employer.

children.heading: Application of certain rules in determination of employer size
children.label: 6 Application of certain rules in determination of employer size

children.heading: Application of aggregation rule for employers
children.label: A Application of aggregation rule for employers
children.text: all persons treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of shall be treated as 1 employer.4section 414 of title 26So in original. Probably should be capitalized.4o

children.heading: Employers not in existence in preceding year
children.label: B Employers not in existence in preceding year
children.text: In the case of an employer which was not in existence throughout the preceding calendar year, the determination of whether such employer is a small or large employer shall be based on the average number of employees that it is reasonably expected such employer will employ on business days in the current calendar year.

children.heading: Predecessors
children.label: C Predecessors
children.text: Any reference in this subsection to an employer shall include a reference to any predecessor of such employer.

children.heading: State option to extend definition of small employer
children.label: 7 State option to extend definition of small employer
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraphs (2) and (4), nothing in this section shall prevent a State from applying this subsection by treating as a small employer, with respect to a calendar year and a plan year, an employer who employed an average of at least 1 but not more than 100 employees on business days during the preceding calendar year and who employs at least 1 employee on the first day of the plan year.
